gitState.notes: Workspace had pre-existing untracked .pan/prd.md before this planning session wrote continue/spec artifacts.

decisions.summary: Plan only the reopened PAN-1201 gaps plus regression protection: add the plural `pan projects` command surface and build the dashboard Context editor/preview page. Existing layered context CLI, templating, workspace assembly, Pi loading, and sync-sources consolidation are treated as already implemented and should not be rewritten unless tests expose a defect.

Implement `pan projects` as a plural alias group that reuses the existing project command handlers. Keep singular `pan project` as a backwards-compatible alias; update user-facing guidance to prefer the plural command required by the issue contract.

Build dashboard Context support as a first-class HTTP route module plus React page, not as frontend-only filesystem assumptions. Server endpoints must use async filesystem APIs and validate every editable path against the global context directory, registered project roots, and workspaces under those projects.

Preserve the current implementation's `.pan/context/{project,workspace}.md` paths rather than reverting to the older PRD wording of `.panopticon/context`. The existing code and PAN-967 project rules place project/workspace lifecycle artifacts under `.pan/`; the dashboard should expose those files.

Use Monaco for the dashboard editor by adding `@monaco-editor/react` and `monaco-editor`, because the PRD explicitly calls for a Monaco editor and the repo currently has no existing code editor dependency to reuse.

Expose Save & Sync from the dashboard by saving layer files through async route handlers, then invoking `pan context sync` out-of-process with an async child process. Do not import `src/lib/sync.ts` into dashboard routes because its context sync implementation still uses sync filesystem calls.

For the Full prompt audit pane, show the complete Panopticon-injected prompt bundle Panopticon can know: rendered global/project/workspace layers plus explicit MEMORY_CONTEXT/status/briefing placeholders or loaded workspace content. Do not pretend to display Claude Code's private base system prompt, which is not available to Panopticon.

hazards.summary: Dashboard server code must not import modules whose route execution path uses readFileSync/writeFileSync/readdirSync/statSync/execSync.
hazards.mitigation: Implement `src/dashboard/server/routes/context.ts` with fs/promises and async child_process helpers. Reuse only pure context-layer functions such as `renderForHarness`/path builders where safe; do not call `renderGlobalLayer`, `renderProjectLayer`, or `syncContextLayersSync` from the route.

hazards.summary: Context save endpoints could otherwise become arbitrary file write primitives.
hazards.mitigation: Contract and route beads must validate layer names and resolve project/workspace selections from the registered project list. Refuse project/workspace paths outside registered roots and only write `global.md`, `project.md`, or `workspace.md`.

hazards.summary: The current code uses `.pan/context` while the original PRD text mentioned `.panopticon/context` for project/workspace layers.
hazards.mitigation: Treat `.pan/context` as the implemented path contract for this reopened work and encode it in dashboard labels/tests/docs so work agents do not split the system across two locations.

hazards.summary: Adding Monaco can affect dashboard bundle/test setup.
hazards.mitigation: Add a small wrapper component that can be mocked in unit tests; verify frontend typecheck/build and ContextPage tests after dependency installation with Bun.

hazards.summary: Save & Sync writes to user/project CLAUDE.md files and must only happen on an explicit user action.
hazards.mitigation: Preview routes are read-only; only the Save & Sync button calls the sync endpoint. Tests should assert preview does not write files.

hazards.summary: Browser verification can leak state if it reuses another agent's Playwright context.
hazards.mitigation: UAT acceptance criteria require an isolated browser/profile and reproducible setup, with no dependence on another agent's browser session.

sessionHistory.note: Read the injected planning instructions from the original sessionHistory[0].content field before discovery.

Discovery found existing PAN-1201 implementation for layered CLI sync, context templating, workspace assembly, Pi loading, and sync-sources consolidation. Reopened issue comment identifies remaining unmet surfaces: plural `pan projects add` and dashboard Context tab/page with editing and previews.
